levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify body parts or privacy concepts.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, names some body parts but shows confusion about privacy concepts.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies most body parts but inconsistently explains which areas are private.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies and names body parts including private areas and clearly explains which parts are private.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Applies body awareness knowledge to explain privacy boundaries in different contexts or situations.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently identifies and names' and 'clearly explains', indicating complete mastery of the learning target.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to communicate personal boundaries.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, attempts to express boundaries but communication is unclear or inconsistent.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently communicates some boundaries about physical contact but struggles with privacy needs or clarity.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Clearly communicates personal boundaries about both physical contact and privacy needs in age-appropriate situations.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Adapts boundary communication effectively across different contexts and supports others in expressing their boundaries.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot communicate boundaries about private areas without first identifying which body parts are private.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated independently with 'clearly communicates' showing successful achievement of the learning target.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to demonstrate relationship skills or safety knowledge.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some relationship skills but applies them inconsistently or inaccurately.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently demonstrates basic relationship skills in familiar contexts but struggles with complex situations.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently applies foundational relationship skills and safety knowledge to build and maintain positive connections with others.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers relationship skills to unfamiliar contexts and integrates safety knowledge with advanced interpersonal strategies.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot build safe relationships without ability to communicate personal boundaries.
Understanding healthy friendship characteristics typically precedes applying broader relationship skills.
Assertive communication and boundary setting are fundamental components of building positive relationships.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor clearly states the learner 'independently applies' the required skills, demonstrating complete achievement of the learning target without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to manage feelings or reflect on behaviors.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some difficult feelings but struggles to manage them appropriately.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently recognises difficult feelings and attempts reflection but responses lack consistency or effectiveness.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently manages difficult feelings and reflects on kind behaviors to navigate challenging relationship situations effectively.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers emotional management skills to novel contexts and supports others in challenging situations.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Basic relationship skills provide foundation for managing complex challenges.
Cannot manage difficult feelings without understanding emotions and mental health concepts.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently manages' and 'effectively', positioning Competent as complete success without hedging language.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify family structures.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, names some family types but descriptions are incomplete or inaccurate.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies several family forms but descriptions lack detail or contain minor gaps.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies and describes various family structures including nuclear, single-parent, blended, extended, adoptive, and same-sex parent families accurately.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Compares family structures across cultures or explains how family diversity strengthens communities.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated independently and accurately, standing alone as evidence that the learning target is met.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify abuse or unsafe situations.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some obvious forms of abuse but misses subtle indicators.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently recognises clear abuse forms but struggles with complex or ambiguous unsafe situations.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies different forms of abuse and recognises when situations are unsafe or harmful.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Identifies abuse patterns and unsafe situations across diverse contexts with sophisticated reasoning.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding body parts and privacy is essential for recognising physical and sexual abuse.
Understanding personal boundaries helps learners recognise when those boundaries are being violated.
Recognising trust and safety risks provides foundational skills for identifying unsafe situations.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor clearly states the learner 'independently identifies' the required capabilities, demonstrating complete achievement of the learning target without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to apply friendship strategies.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, attempts some friendship strategies but application is inconsistent or inappropriate.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently applies basic friendship strategies but struggles when challenges become complex or unfamiliar.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently applies effective strategies to build caring friendships and responds appropriately to friendship challenges.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers friendship strategies to diverse contexts and helps others navigate complex friendship situations.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot apply friendship strategies without understanding what healthy friendships look like.
Foundational relationship skills provide the base for more specific friendship management strategies.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated independently and can stand alone as evidence that the learning target is met.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to communicate needs or set boundaries.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, attempts to express needs but struggles with clarity or emotional regulation.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Communicates some needs and boundaries independently but inconsistently manages emotions during challenging interactions.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Communicates assertively to express needs and set appropriate boundaries while effectively managing emotions in interpersonal situations.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Adapts assertive communication strategies flexibly across diverse contexts while supporting others' boundary-setting efforts.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot set boundaries assertively without first understanding how to communicate personal boundaries.
Understanding emotions supports effective emotional management during assertive communication.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated at the learning target's level of demand without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify bullying types or stereotypes.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, names some bullying types but explanations are incomplete or inaccurate.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies basic bullying types and stereotypes but explanations of harmful impacts lack detail.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies different types of bullying and stereotypes and explains their harmful impacts accurately and clearly.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Analyses complex bullying scenarios and connects stereotypes to broader patterns of discrimination and harm.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding positive friendship characteristics helps distinguish bullying from normal relationship challenges.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated independently and accurately, standing alone as evidence that the learning target is met.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to challenge stereotypes or seek help.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some stereotypes or help-seeking options but responses are incomplete.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently recognises stereotypes or identifies help sources but challenges or responses lack effectiveness.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently challenges stereotypes effectively and seeks appropriate help when bullying occurs using suitable strategies and channels.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers challenging skills to novel contexts and advocates for others facing bullying situations.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot challenge what you cannot identify or understand.
Assertive communication skills support effective challenging of stereotypes.
General help-seeking skills provide foundation for bullying-specific responses.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently' and 'effectively' without any hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify online risks or safety principles.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, names some online risks but explanations are incomplete or inaccurate.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies basic online risks and explains simple safety principles with minor gaps.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies diverse online risks and explains comprehensive safety principles for protecting personal information and navigating digital relationships.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Applies safety principles to novel scenarios and evaluates emerging digital risks beyond standard contexts.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding personal boundaries supports recognizing digital boundary violations and privacy needs.
Recognizing unsafe situations generally helps identify when online interactions become harmful or inappropriate.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated independently and comprehensively, standing alone as evidence that the learning target is met.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to evaluate online threats or responses.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some obvious online risks but responses are inappropriate or incomplete.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently recognises clear online threats but struggles to evaluate complex situations or select effective responses.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Critically evaluates online relationships and content, identifies harmful situations accurately, and applies appropriate response strategies independently.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers evaluation skills to novel online contexts and helps others develop threat recognition capabilities.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot evaluate online threats without understanding digital risks and safety principles.
General ability to recognise unsafe situations supports online threat identification.
Help-seeking skills are typically established before applying response strategies to online threats.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated and independently achieved at the learning target's level of demand without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify trustworthy people or unsafe situations.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some obvious trust or safety indicators but makes frequent errors.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently recognises clear trustworthy behaviours and obvious unsafe situations but misses subtle warning signs.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies trustworthy versus untrustworthy people and recognises unsafe situations or inappropriate contact across various contexts.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Applies trust and safety recognition skills to complex or ambiguous situations with sophisticated reasoning.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding private body parts is essential for recognising inappropriate contact.
Understanding personal boundaries supports recognition of boundary violations by others.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently identifies' and 'recognises' without any hedging language or positioning as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to report concerns or seek help.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, attempts to communicate safety concerns using limited vocabulary or inappropriate channels.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies need for help but uses unclear vocabulary or selects inappropriate reporting channels.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently reports safety concerns and seeks appropriate help using clear vocabulary and proper channels consistently.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Adapts reporting strategies to complex situations and guides others in seeking appropriate help effectively.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Must recognise unsafe situations before being able to report them.
Must identify abuse and unsafe situations to know what requires reporting.
Understanding rights and support systems typically precedes learning specific reporting procedures.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently' and 'consistently' indicating complete mastery of the learning target.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to analyse relationship challenges or influences.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some stereotypes or influences but analysis lacks depth or accuracy.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies relationship challenges and influences but evaluation remains superficial or strategies are basic.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Evaluates how stereotypes, power imbalances, and cultural influences affect relationship dynamics and applies effective strategies for managing conflict and endings.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers evaluation skills to complex scenarios and integrates multiple strategies for relationship challenges fluently.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot evaluate relationship dynamics without understanding healthy relationship foundations.
Understanding stereotypes generally precedes analysing their specific impact on relationships.
Managing basic relationship challenges builds foundation for analysing complex relationship dynamics.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated at the learning target's level of demand without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to communicate consent or boundaries.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some consent concepts but communicates boundaries inconsistently or inaccurately.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Communicates basic consent and boundaries in familiar situations but struggles with complex or unfamiliar relationship contexts.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently recognises, respects and communicates consent and boundaries effectively in early romantic and sexual relationships.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers consent communication skills to novel contexts and supports others in understanding boundary dynamics.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot communicate consent and boundaries without foundational boundary communication skills.
Assertive communication is essential for effectively communicating consent and boundaries.
Understanding healthy relationship foundations typically precedes applying consent communication in romantic contexts.
Cannot communicate sexual consent without understanding its fundamental principles.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently' and 'effectively' indicating complete mastery of the learning target.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to explain sexual consent principles.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some consent concepts but explanations are incomplete or inaccurate.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Explains basic consent principles but misses key aspects like withdrawal or timing flexibility.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Explains that sexual consent can be given, withheld, or removed at any time with accurate understanding.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Connects consent principles to broader ethical frameworks and real-world relationship scenarios.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding personal boundaries is essential before grasping consent principles.
General relationship foundations including consent concepts typically precede specific sexual consent principles.
Practical consent communication skills often develop alongside theoretical understanding.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated with accurate understanding, standing alone as evidence the learning target is met.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to evaluate sexual health information.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some accurate information but struggles to counter misinformation effectively.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently evaluates basic sexual health information but inconsistently identifies or addresses misinformation.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently evaluates medically accurate sexual health information and effectively counters misinformation with appropriate signposting.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers evaluation skills to complex contexts and proactively educates others about misinformation.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot evaluate sexual health information without understanding the foundational knowledge being evaluated.
Critical evaluation of online content provides transferable skills for evaluating health information.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently evaluates' and 'effectively counters' without any hedging language or positioning as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify emotions or mental health concepts.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, names basic emotions but shows limited understanding of mental health concepts.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies common emotions and describes some mental health concepts but explanations lack detail.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies range of emotions, explains mental health concepts clearly, and describes factors supporting or harming wellbeing.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Connects emotions to mental health concepts and analyses complex wellbeing factors across different contexts.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated independently and can stand alone as evidence that the learning target is met.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to assess emotional responses or mental health needs.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies basic emotions but struggles to judge when help is needed.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Recognises obvious emotional distress and communicates about mental health with some prompting or guidance.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently assesses emotional responses, judges when support is needed, and communicates appropriately about mental health concerns.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Applies mental health assessment skills to complex situations and supports others in seeking help.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot assess emotional responses without understanding emotions and mental health concepts.
Help-seeking skills are typically established before applying them to mental health contexts.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently' performing all required actions without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify online risks or benefits.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, names some online risks or benefits but explanations are incomplete.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies basic online risks and benefits but struggles to explain wellbeing impacts.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies various online risks and benefits and explains their specific impacts on personal wellbeing.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Analyses complex digital scenarios and evaluates nuanced impacts on different aspects of wellbeing.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding wellbeing concepts is essential to explain impacts on wellbeing.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated independently and can stand alone as evidence that the learner has met the learning target.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to evaluate online content or relationships.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some online risks but makes decisions without clear evaluation.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently recognises basic online risks and benefits but evaluation lacks depth or consistency.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently evaluates online content and relationships using clear criteria to make informed decisions about digital engagement.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers evaluation skills to novel digital contexts and helps others make informed decisions.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot evaluate digital decisions without understanding digital risks and impacts.
Understanding basic digital safety principles typically precedes making complex digital decisions.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently evaluates' and 'make informed decisions' without any hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify health facts.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, names some health facts but demonstrates inaccurate understanding.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies basic health facts but shows gaps in nutrition or hygiene knowledge.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies key facts about nutrition, physical activity, substance use, hygiene, and health maintenance practices accurately.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Connects health facts across domains and explains relationships between different health practices.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Body awareness typically precedes understanding health maintenance practices.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated independently and accurately, standing alone as evidence that the learning target is met.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify healthcare services or access methods.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, names some healthcare services but provides incomplete or inaccurate access information.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies basic healthcare services and explains simple access methods but lacks detail for complex needs.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies appropriate healthcare services for different needs and clearly explains how to access them effectively.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Evaluates healthcare service options and explains access strategies for complex or specialised healthcare needs.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding when to seek health support precedes knowing how to navigate specific healthcare services.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently identifies' and 'clearly explains' without any hedging language or positioning as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to explain medical consent frameworks.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some basic consent concepts but explanations are incomplete or inaccurate.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Explains basic medical consent principles but gaps remain in understanding age-related capacity requirements.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Explains the legal framework for medical consent including age-related capacity and competency requirements accurately and independently.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Applies consent framework knowledge to analyse complex scenarios involving capacity assessment and legal considerations.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding general consent principles is essential before learning specific medical consent frameworks.
Understanding personal rights and legal protections provides foundation for medical consent legal frameworks.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated accurately and independently, standing alone as evidence that the learning target is met.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify hazards or assess risks.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies obvious hazards but struggles to assess actual risk levels.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies common hazards but assessment of risk levels lacks depth or accuracy.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies potential hazards across different environments and accurately assesses their associated safety risks.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Systematically evaluates complex risk scenarios and prioritises multiple hazards by severity and likelihood.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding unsafe situations provides foundation for broader hazard identification skills.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently identifies' and 'accurately assesses' without any hedging language or implications of incompleteness.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to implement safety strategies or seek help.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some safety strategies but struggles to apply them effectively.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently applies basic safety strategies but inconsistently manages peer influence or help-seeking.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently implements appropriate safety strategies, manages peer influence effectively, and seeks help when facing safety concerns.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers safety management skills to novel situations and supports others in developing safety strategies.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot implement safety strategies without first identifying hazards and risks.
Recognising unsafe situations typically precedes learning to manage them.
Basic help-seeking skills provide foundation for broader safety management.
Expressing boundaries supports managing peer influence in safety contexts.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor clearly states the learner independently demonstrates all required capabilities without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to explain physical changes during puberty.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some physical changes but explanations are incomplete or inaccurate.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies physical changes during puberty but explanations lack detail or contain minor gaps.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently explains the physical changes that occur during puberty and adolescence accurately and comprehensively.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Explains puberty changes and connects them to individual variation, timing differences, or broader development.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Must know body parts and anatomy before understanding how they change during puberty.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated independently and comprehensively, standing alone as clear evidence that the learning target is met.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify health issues or strategies.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, recognises some obvious health concerns but applies strategies inaccurately.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies clear health issues and applies basic strategies but misses subtle concerns.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies when health issues require attention and applies appropriate strategies to protect wellbeing effectively.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Transfers health identification skills to complex situations and integrates multiple protective strategies.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Cannot identify when health issues require attention without understanding basic health knowledge.
Understanding how to seek health support typically precedes applying broader wellbeing strategies.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as fully demonstrated with 'independently identifies' and 'applies appropriate strategies...effectively' without any hedging language or positioning as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to explain rights or support systems.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some basic rights but explanations are incomplete or inaccurate.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Explains some personal rights and support options but misses key legal protections or help-seeking processes.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Explains personal rights, legal protections, and appropriate help-seeking processes when safety is compromised independently and accurately.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Applies rights knowledge to complex scenarios and evaluates effectiveness of different support systems.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding healthy relationships provides foundation for recognizing when rights are violated.
Seeking help when safety is compromised requires the capability to seek appropriate support.

levels.name: developing

levels.name: competent

levels.name: extending

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to explain relationship foundations.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some relationship elements but explanations are incomplete or inaccurate.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Explains some key elements like consent or boundaries but misses connections between concepts.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Explains how consent, boundaries, equality, and power dynamics create respectful, ethical romantic relationships independently.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Connects relationship foundations to broader social contexts or applies principles to complex scenarios.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to explain sexual health concepts.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, identifies some contraceptive or health terms but explanations are inaccurate.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Explains basic contraceptive options or STI prevention but lacks detail on pregnancy choices.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Explains contraceptive options, pregnancy choices, STI prevention methods, and key reproductive health facts accurately and independently.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Connects sexual health knowledge to broader health decisions and evaluates information sources critically.

Understanding healthy relationship foundations provides essential context for sexual health discussions.

rubrics.competent_framing_judge_rationale: The descriptor declares the capability as demonstrated accurately and independently, matching the learning target's requirements without hedging language or positioning it as incomplete.

levels.name: no_evidence
levels.descriptor: No attempt to identify health support needs or sources.

levels.name: emerging
levels.descriptor: With support, recognises some health concerns but struggles to identify appropriate help sources.

levels.name: developing
levels.descriptor: Independently identifies when health support is needed but shows gaps in selecting appropriate sources.

levels.name: competent
levels.descriptor: Independently determines when health support is needed and identifies appropriate trusted adults or healthcare professionals to approach.

levels.name: extending
levels.descriptor: Evaluates multiple health support options and adapts approach based on specific circumstances or urgency.

prerequisite_edges.rationale: Understanding help-seeking processes generally supports health-specific help-seeking skills.
